reviews.review_description:
My review scale*
5- Amazing game, while still might not be perfect any flaws are forgivable, is well worth your time
4- Good game, might have a few minor issues or missed oppurtunities keeping it from being better, but is very playable and still an enjoyable experience
3- Mid tier game, not great but not awful, these games can go either way depending on who you are or what you want or expect out of the game
2- Not a very good game, but not completely broken or unplayable, you might really need to want to play this game to get any enjoyment out of it
1- Awful game, broken or unplayable, I dont see these games being for anyone unless significant updates fix the game

*This is an overly simplified review scale since meta does not allow for half stars so in some cases I may round a game up or down depending on a case by case scenario, 
my reviews are always honest and in no way swayed by anybody else and regardless if I recieved a game for free or not, these ratings and reviews are however part of a 
campaign for us as consumers to be better about reviewing games and helping bring the games we love the attention they deserve.
This game is action packed and will jeep moving and dodging and keeping you on your toes. I love the shield you can pull from you back
Lots of energy in this classic VR game. A must have.

Being new to VR, I was searching for a game that  would be both easy to use and action packed. I tried around 10 shooter games before I found Space Pirate Trainer. The entertainment Space Pirate Trainer provides by far exceeds any other shooting games I’ve tried. There is just something extremely satisfying about being in outer space and shooting alien robots with lasers, grenades and an assortment of other exciting weapons.  Almost every time I enter Space Pirate Trainer in VR, I end up playing until my headset and battery die, which usually lasts around 5 hours. It is fun to use in any space, small or large, but large spaces seem to accommodate better than small. Regardless, it’s a blast!
Very fun its kinda action packed play it like everyday its fun when you are bored. I recommend!

This is the best that it’s so much fun that you can shoot some enemies with your guns or you could change them and even the coolest thing that you could use a shield and a sword can you

￼

The bosses are so fun I love the arena that comes up at I think it's level 46
I wish there was an Easter mode the amount of times I crashed out at this game is amazing five stars

reviews.review_title: Intensive and Fun as nothing else
reviews.review_description:

reviews.review_title: This game is still alive but sadly...
reviews.review_description: This game is still alive but sadly it is not updated... I´m still enjoying it .

It's a very old game by VR standards. I remember this being one of the first games I played when I first got my original HTC Vive Headset. The game has been updated a bit, but not a ton. It still holds up okay. You shoot things in the sky and try and dodge the bullets. It does get pretty hard fast. Classic arcade type where you progress in waves as each round gets harder. Not much to this game, but what it offers it works well. Since it's on Quest+ right now, it's a good game to waste a few minutes in, or easy enough to show off to new VR players.

reviews.review_title: Fun game but could use another update
reviews.review_description: Hey devs this game is fantastic and alot of dodging skill but maybe if possible could put more weapons in and like more skins to add on ur weapon PLEASE READ THIS

Fun, well thought out exciting game play has turned into buggy, laggy lazy garbage. When I first started playing this game, I couldn't get enough. Every map was a new and involved adventure that was thoughtful, themed, well designed and well written. What it's become is a laggy, bug ridden, lazily written spooge fest. No themes or original stories or content. In fact? It just seems like all content has become is stacking endless nests on top of each other as if the creators want to do nothing but make it impossible for you to complete a map. There's fun, there's challenging and then there's the garbage I'm seeing in Guardians frontline nowadays. Absolutely no thought seems to go into creating original ideas or strategies.  It's sad because I used to play this game non-stop. This was the game I introduced my friends to VR with. Now? It's a pale shadow of the awesome FPS it once was. It's actually turned me off to playing VR anymore at all. It seems like with each subsequent update, the game gets MORE laggy with MORE bugs instead of the other way around. I've honestly never seen games like this get WORSE with updates! If I don't see any improvements soon? I'm probably going to shelve it permanently.  There's ZERO new content. I have purchased EVERY piece of gear and upgrade available and am quite close to pulling the plug completely.  FIX MY FAVORITE GAME PLEASE!!???
This one is still the best
This is a fun game, makes me laugh and jump around trying to duck out of the way of the lasers, I really like the drum n bass music too
Man, this game is the bomb! Definitely worth the play. Wish there was more levels tho.

Really boring shooter. You shoot fast moving balls with very very slow bullets. Also no graphics, just pitch black background and floating balls lol. Iirc there is no music. 


Of all games of this genre, it's THE worst one and hilariously overpriced

Great game play but would benefit from an MR update to allow limited pass through of surroundings. Blending real surroundings with the fantastic game elements will definitely make this a great game and a good workout! I kept banging on stuff but allowing MR Will make this more useful for that purpose.
This game is just absolutely brilliant. A good way to get moving stood or sat. So much endless fun.

This game made some of the best controls in a semi-stationary position for arcade shooters and created a masterpiece. 

I love the different guns, shield, weapons and when you really get into it, you can lose track of time and feel like a complete sci-fi master pirate.

reviews.review_title: Arena mode will NOT work on quest2/3
reviews.review_description:
First off...know ahead of time you need a 30' x 30' clear area to play arena. That's HUGE! That's not the problem for my nephew and I. The issue is, you can't create the space needed.

There's no way to create a 30' x 30' boundary for you to be able to play the arena portion of this game. That's a limitation of the quest boundary setup. Neither the Quest 2/3/3s can create such a huge boundary for the type of play this game requires.

There are ZERO instructions ( even in YouTube ) to setup this huge play area through any kind of manipulation, either.  Zero. It's not as though you can create multiple boundaries in your VR headset either, and stitch them together or anything, so what gives here? 

Very frustrating because you see everyone in trailers or on sponsored YouTube videos playing this but zero clue on how you get past the boundary limitations so, I believe this one must be returned. Outside of that exact mode, there's no reason to play this one. We want PVP in an arena, not two players shooting at random floating targets in vs mode.

Very unfortunate, I'd say.

If the developers have any idea or instructions for how to do this, please share. I've got two copies for no reason if not. Do you have a video instruction of how you do this? It's not a simple " boundary " set-up...so do you have a " set-up video " of some sort?

Buyer be aware, I'd say. Not saying this game is no good or anything...I'm saying you won't be able to play it without knowing how to do  something that your headset isn't capable of doing. Be aware that you need a space larger than two empty living rooms as well. Be aware that there's no video tutorial of how to set this up either.
I don’t usually rates/review games but this one earned it. 10/10 would recommend. Super fun
Uncomplicated game which allows me to shoot targets (not people) quite whenever I choose, then get back to real life without feeling that I’ve abandoned the mission

reviews.review_title: If you want this game 15% cheaper use th
reviews.review_description: Nice game read the title

TL,DR: Game itself is good, but the joystick-only weapon selection makes it a no go for me.

I originally played SPT in an arcade, and it was quite good on a Vive device. When the Quest version came out, I got excited to play it and threw my cash away that instant, only to be disappointed almost immediately. Why, you ask? Joysticks. All. Just. Joysticks. There are no other options for weapon selection other than joysticks. You can’t press one of the face buttons and shift the controller to a position like in Project Demigod, nor can you select 3 weapons per controller and swap to them by pressing a corresponding button or clicking the joystick. I absolutely despise games that force you to use joysticks, and this game could have been an easy 10/10 “you have absolutely no excuse to not immediately buy this” game, but they had to ruin it by forcing you to use the Quest Touch controller’s finicky(and near-instantly breakable)joysticks. Joysticks drifting? Too bad, so sad; maybe just buy another controller pair altogether. I’m not even sure why you’d want to be fiddling with those stupid rubber knobs during an intense drone shootout anyhow, ‘cause your hands would be sweating like crazy making it near-impossible to switch weapons. Once again, it’d be much easier to have that radial menu-calling button available as at least a secondary option for those with drifting sticks. To keep this from becoming even more of a rant than it already is, no thanks mate, I’ll be trying my luck with the Steam version.

reviews.review_description: I'm enjoying playing this update to a classic Space Invaders/Galaxians. It's a shame a 10mx10m boundary is required to play arena, because I'm sure a smaller 8mx8m would be just as much fun and could open up the second half of this game to more users.
What can I say other that the game has potential but the distracting music keep me from enjoying it at all!!

Now it's stuck in the opening and won't load. What's the deal?

I love this game, but the devs have abandoned it. There are no upgrades. I mean, this game is one of the best games in meta! What happened to you guys? Please! Upgrade the game with different stuff. Come on!

reviews.review_title: 💫 SP review and free PRO $ Codes ✌️
reviews.review_description:
Space Pirate Trainer DX is a VR game that lets you experience the thrill of being a space pirate in various modes and environments. You can shoot, dodge, and deflect waves of droids in the classic mode, or challenge your friends in the new versus and arena modes. Here is a possible review for Space Pirate Trainer DX:

Space Pirate Trainer DX is a blast from the past, but with a futuristic twist. It is a game that combines the arcade-style wave shooter with the immersive and interactive VR technology. It is a game that will test your reflexes, accuracy, and strategy as you face different types of droids and environments.


Some of the pros and cons of Space Pirate Trainer DX are:

Pros:

1. It is a fun and addictive game that will keep you entertained for hours.

2. It has a variety of weapons and power-ups to suit your play style and preferences.

3. It has a simple and intuitive user interface and controls that are easy to use and navigate.

4. It has a high-quality graphics and sound that create a realistic and immersive VR experience.

5. It has a multiplayer mode that adds a social and competitive aspect to the game.

6. It has an arena mode that offers a unique and exhilarating VR laser tag experience.


Cons:

1. It can be repetitive and monotonous after a while, as the game does not have much variety or depth in terms of gameplay and content.

2. It can be challenging and frustrating at times, as the game can be very difficult and unforgiving, especially in the later levels.

3. It can be buggy and glitchy at times, especially when there are many players or objects in the game.

developer_response.body:
This is the review area for Space Pirate Trainer DX, a game developed by I-Illusions (that's us), not for hardware complaints. 

For the sake of the other independent developers out there who's very livelihoods depend on star ratings, I hope you haven't done this with any other of the games you have purchased.

For hardware support, please contact Meta here https://support.oculus.com

reviews.review_description: Is there anyway to do arena wothout having a 30x30ft area?  I'm not sure how that would be feasible for anyone to have that much open space.  Without that it's basically a demo game that was a waste of money

developer_response.body: Arena mode was added as a bonus feature to the core Space Pirate Trainer game, take it to a basketball court, gym or community hall and transform the space into an epic gaming experience. At home there's the all new Space Pirate Trainer Versus mode, which is suitable in all play spaces. Try it out!

reviews.review_description:
BUNDLES CANT BE REFUNDED (incase you're here at the time it's in bundle)
THE GAME THEY SHOW IN VIDEO NEEDS 10X10M INDOOR SPACE TO PLAY.

Sorry but this is misleading ,I'd say it's a great big HAM, the game you get sold in the video is a game most will likely never play unless you have an indoor tennis court, what you get sold in the video isn't the main game ,the main games an old wave shooter game that's been done a thousand times before  , the game you see in the sales video,  the one they choose to represent the game,  the one you want to play needs a 10x10m indoor space 🤦‍♂️ So be warned if you're buying the bundle ,because you can't get refunds on bundles ,I basically paid £24 for hyperdash ,which is an amazing game but I could of bought it for £15 and I'd be even happier.

Edit--- Response --- if the advert was sold to me as , look at this amazing wave shooter for £xxx and we've added this one of a kind arena game....for free 😱, the arena game needs X amount of space so you can't play it all the time but it's worth the effort, and days when you don't have the space you still have this OG wave shooter to play.
I actually think I'd feel like I was getting a better deal, 2 for 1, rather than feeling like I'd been duped.

developer_response.body:
Well, we actually didn't know you can't refund a title that's in a bundle.
Arena was a free update for everyone that had SPT, and specifically made for large play spaces. We're discussing internally how to make this more clear, maybe combine both trailers and add more info (we can't add two trailers on the store). The last thing we want is unhappy customers.
Also... Just FYI, this IS the OG VR wave shooter, not a copy or clone. We've just updated it along the way and ported it to different platforms. Arena mode is only available on Quest.

reviews.review_description:
I have never written a review but I need to vent my frustrations or I will internally combust!!

I bought this for the Arena. So it’s suppose to be THE game to bring VR gameplay out of the room, into the open! All you need is a personal hotspot from your mobile phone and a 10mx10m space. Sounds easy enough, right?

WRONG! 

Now I am no techie so this could be a hardware problem (hint hint Oculus) or a software one, but my money is on BOTH! 

Firstly u need a 10m x 10m space and ideally u should rent an indoor court to play this but hey, an open outdoor secure place should suffice too, right?!
So first u draw yr guardian boundary and then u load up the game and enter the Arena mode, then BAM! Even if u r short of 1cm (!), u cannot play Arena and guess what, u will need to REDRAW the boundary!
Why can’t the developers have an in-game adjustment feature?!! 

Secondly being outdoors and relying on a hotspot, I have wasted hours just trying to draw a 10m x 10m boundary! I don’t know what’s the issue, network? Connectivity between headset and controller?! Oculus! What’s the problem?! I tried wiping the headset cameras too. But it just won’t draw at certain spots and the game won’t let u play Arena mode even if u r 1cm off!!

Sounds like u really do need a perfect indoor well lit court and with wifi connection to play this.

So BE WARNED!!!

developer_response.body:
Actually, we check for 9.5 x 9.5m when you're trying to play Arena mode, just to give some extra room.
And unfortunately, we can't adjust the guardian in our game, it's just not available in the API. We're working with Oculus to find some solutions in the near future.
We can try to help: support@i-illusions.com

reviews.review_description: This game was my favorite. But with all the bugs (especially in versus) it makes it not so enjoyable anymore. Plus they don’t explain how to unlock certain features as in gold or neon guns etc. Hopefully they make the necessary changes to make this game great again.

developer_response.body: Hey there! Sorry you're experiencing issues with versus. Are you able to send me an email and we can try and see what's going on support@i-illusions.com? Cheers!

reviews.review_description:
Was charged  twice  for  the  space pirates game
 Need a refund. John

developer_response.body:
Hi John, unfortunately 1 star reviews won't help with purchase issues, they'll just make other players less confident in Space Pirate Trainer DX. 
To resolve your payment issue please contact Oculus (https://www.oculus.com/legal/quest-rift-content-refund-policy/) and re-consider your 1 star review. 

Thanks, Chris and the Space Pirates

reviews.review_description:
I can’t even play the game because the guardian system won’t allow me to create a big enough guardian and the devs thought it was smart to not allow you to play the game if you disable the guardian. If I disable the guardian that should let you know I obviously have a big enough space. This is a dumb feature. If I wasn’t in the 14 day limit I would get a refund. I’m definitely going to promote against downloading this game until something is done.

Response to Devs: Someone getting hurt is completely on the user and not you. My feelings remain the same.

developer_response.body:
You can't play Arena mode you mean? Because SPT should work fine.
We can't allow disabling the guardian for arena, because we don't want people getting hurt in our game. Disabling the guardian doesn't mean that you're in a big enough space. You could be standing in your kitchen for all we know. If you have the space, you should be able to draw it. if you're facing rotational problems, you can easily reset the guardian direction (by switching to stationary guardian and back to room-scale while looking in the right direction).

reviews.review_description:
Stop celebrating this update, it's awful. Arena mode is not even playable for most people. "Turn any indoor space" is a complete lie. It requires 10meters by 10meters of space. An above average completely empty master bedroom is half that size. Who and where are you expecting people to play this? This update is rediculous. Why even bother with it? Most VR arcades (may they rest in peace) couldn't even support that size requirement. Are you expecting location based VR or something? During a pandemic? What a poorly executed let down. But with all the Oculus store hype, you at least made space for ego. I wonder if the arena game is even any good, I'll never know I guess.

Let's put this in perspective, you're asking for over 1,000sq ft of space to play arena mode. And you can't even bother to put that hard requirement in the details? You've been in VR so long you've lost touch with reality.

For some reason my Oculus Quest 2 will NOT let me define a guardian area larger than 10 m x 10 m. I tried doing this outside at dusk. I have the hard and got something like 8 x 8 m (?) but won't let me complete drawing the boundaries.

Can the devs help me figure what I'm asking wrong?

developer_response.body: can you email us at support@i-illusions.com? If you draw the edges of the guardian, it should work fine.

reviews.review_title: You guys really are something else.
reviews.review_description:
You've some nerve hyping up this DX upgrade for years, and think people have a freaking basketball court to play vr in. You didn't do ANYTHING to the base experience. Same UI. Same music. Same stupid game.

But then, lo and behold all the DX content with its flashy graphics and new weapons and new maps and new textures new content is locked away in this esoteric little mode. 

It couldn't even spill over into the base experience in the slightest? How  privileged are you guys? You've no capacity for empathy in your player base at all. You are so beyond out of touch with us it's out of this world. When I spoofed my guardian to go into arena and loaded into that menu I wanted to throw up. All that effort went into that mode. 

You have all this content and you bury it in the mode to such a degree it's almost like you spite the original game. Like we're trash for loving it. It's so far removed from the base game it literally has sharper graphics and new sound. But who the hell is going to play it? You little rich snobs really didn't deserve my ten dollars. You definitely shouldn't get your fifteen. 

I hope you enjoy all the angry refunds from people with literally regular lives, with apartments and roommates, who work in offices and whom aren't outside with their VR headsets or looking for friends to join them at the handball court at the gym. 

Oh actually nvm, most of those would be too small to play Arena. 10m by 10m? You guys have to be the worst VR game designers of all time. Echo Arena manages to create an arena experience tripling this distance that paraplegic participants can play in their own homes. This sucks. It's shameful.

Base game for original price is great.
Subsidizing the Arena update by hike the price when most players can't play it is gross.

So many examples of smaller space VR that do this (Tae for God anyone).
Arena is very cool. Glad it exists. Not happy with how it's executed currently.
Downloaded the game and it was awesome but now will not load at all....they need to fix this. Till then 1 star rating for a broken game.

developer_response.body: Can  you contact us at support@i-illusions.com? This shouldn't happen

reviews.review_description: It's a super fun arcade style game best played in a BIG area to enable dodging and outmaneuvering your enemies. This game is a workout!

reviews.review_title: Give Me Your Money, Figure It Out
reviews.review_description:
There seems to be loads of different types of guns, shields, power-ups, etc. A normal developer would introduce each one of these mechanics one by one, letting the casual gamer increase in skill as the game increases in difficulty. Not this developer. This developer says now that we have your money, you’re on your own. “Figure it out, numb-skull,” this developer says. A short tutorial basically shows you two of the games many mechanics. Want to know how to change guns? “Figure it out,” they say. Want to know what all of the icons mean? “We don’t care.” Want to know how the shield and light-saber work? “That’s what YouTube is for.” What do the ammo lights on the guns communicate? “Shut-up, sucker.” Hey, what is Old School mode? “Just because we put it in there, we don’t have to tell you what it is!” Are you a casual gamer looking for an updated version of the classics? “Move on, Boomer.” This is but a partial list of things the developer left out. I guess they haven’t figured out that it’s basically free to put some text in a game. 

Quest 2 is supposed to be for both hardcore and casual gamers. Unless you are a hardcore gamer or want to go digging online for instruction, guides, and tips, avoid this game. Could have been amazing if the developers had less contempt for newbies. When will developers figure out, that’s a game being frustrating because you don’t know how things work is NOT fun. There is NOTHING fun about putting in hours without progressing, because you still don’t know the basic rules. I guess if you make the user invest hours learning the very basics, you’ll have to give out less refunds. I have over 20 O2 games, many both fun AND hard. But those games aren’t hard for lack of a decent manual or guide. This is the first time a game provided no DECENT tutorial or guide to what’s-what.
